title: PostgreSQL query fails due to incorrect quoting
symptom: Executing a query in the PostgreSQL node results in an error stating 'column “history” does not exist'. This occurs when trying to call a function with incorrectly quoted parameters.
applies_when: Executing a query that calls a Supabase function
not_this_if: The query executes successfully without errors
root_cause: In PostgreSQL, identifiers (like column names) should be enclosed in double quotes, while string literals should be enclosed in single quotes. Using double quotes for string literals leads to the error indicating that the column does not exist.
fix_steps: Change the double quotes around the string parameter to single quotes
agent_instruction: Replace double quotes with single quotes around string parameters in the query.
